module_mood_contract: Unleashed leverage — 'We can automate the loop.' Empowered builder returns; human moves one level up and designs the loop, the standards, and the autonomy boundary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured.
Epistemic mood not punitive.
Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
Strategy-fidelity: no front-running downstream teaching moments.
Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 39-43: Key Concepts bullets name the module's tactics well (eval as infrastructure, role separation, walk-away autonomy, yardstick integrity, loop ownership) but carry no 'Remember: <theme essence>' breadcrumbs. No bullet surfaces the training-wide themes (compounding, epistemic discipline, the new human role) in theme-keyed language.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Remember:' breadcrumb to 1-2 bullets that map to the training's recurring themes — e.g. on 'The work gets sharper because the judge stays still': 'Remember: the yardstick has to stay still for the score to mean anything.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not a standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 39-43: No breadcrumbs present. Paired finding with rule 5.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Same fix as rule 5 — add 1-2 breadcrumbs in-line on existing bullets; do not add a standalone Themes section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 28: [Lecture: Evals as steering] placed before exercise — correctly classified as an arming lecture (sets up the fixed-judge concept), not a meta-frame/recognition lecture. line 36: [Closing: The new human role in the loop] placed after exercise — correctly a closer. Strategy doc confirms: 'Evals as steering leads with Mollick's frame and sets up the fixed-judge loop' (arming); 'New Human Role names what M1-M6 just built' (recognition/closer).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 47-59: Debrief prompt encodes all forcing functions (read tactic, scan runs, name what changed, name one boundary case). Body prose after the prompt (lines 62-63) offers optional push-back examples ('That rule is too vague, make it observable') — these are optional refinement moves, not mandatory forcing functions; the 2026-05-02 carve-out applies.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text. No authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Scanned full body: no 'share with your neighbor', 'tell the room', 'compare with the person next to you', 'discuss with...' or equivalent cohort-management sentences found.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+): student leaves the chair while the agent works.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 26-26: 'What if you weren't in the room while that happened?' — walk-away is central to the module's design. Maintainer block confirms 'walk-away duration: default 25-30 min.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Eval is a fixed yardstick; the thing measured is what learns.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 42-43: 'The judge doesn't move. That's why round 3's score has anything to say about round 1's. A yardstick you rewrite is not a yardstick.' — integrity check is explicit and repeated.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 49: 'Ask Claude to read the round trail and sharpen the generator's tactic beyond what the loop reached.' — present, under 20 words.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape: lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 51-59: **Prompt** *(Claude Code)* with blank line before fenced block. Shape is correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks.
Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths (Bootstrap-specific).
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 54-58: ./generation-tactic.md, module-6/runs/, module-6/eval-notes.md, judges/groundedness-judge.md — all explicit subpaths.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands: 8+/10 at every beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[0].mood_score=8 (leverage lands; slight logistics drag in Start here keeps it from 9). trace.close.mood_score=9 ('Trust the loop you can inspect' is the right landing).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 is the final core module; leverage is the target mood and not resolved prematurely. Handoff line ('trust the loop you can inspect') correctly defers M7 generosity rather than collapsing it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment ('the judge stays fixed — that's the integrity of the loop') is stated in Big Idea, in body prose (lines 26-26), in Key Concepts (lines 42-43), and enforced structurally in the exercise design. Cannot be skipped by a speed-runner.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity: file honors the strategy doc's Big Idea framing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 3-7: Big Idea names the fixed judge, the sharpening generator, the walk-away, and the 'yardstick' integrity — all matching strategy doc M6 ('eval as fixed yardstick / leverage'). No competing framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: close hands off to M7 open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 64-66 (Next): 'trust the loop you can inspect' — closes M6's leverage arc without pre-empting M7's generosity impulse. Strategy doc M7 open: 'this is starting to work; I wonder if others could benefit?' — not resolved or stolen by M6's close.
